paper_title: CTRL: A Conditional Transformer Language Model for Controllable Generation

raw_extraction.innovation: CTRL introduces a conditional transformer language model conditioned on control codes derived from natural text structures (e.g., URLs, domain tags), allowing explicit control over generated text in terms of domain, style, content, entities, relationships, and task-specific behavior
raw_extraction.pretraining_corpus: 140 GB of text from Wikipedia (En, De, Es, Fr), Project Gutenberg, 45 subreddits, OpenWebText, news data, Amazon Reviews, Europarl/UN data (En-De, En-Es, En-Fr), question-answer pairs from ELI5 and MRQA shared task
raw_extraction.application: Controllable text generation, domain-specific text creation, task-specific generation (e.g., question answering, translation), source attribution analysis
raw_extraction.research_problem: Lack of explicit control over generated text in large language models
raw_extraction.pretraining_task: Causal language modeling with conditional control codes
raw_extraction.finetuning_task: None specified (fully pre-trained with conditional generation)
raw_extraction.extension: Control codes are derived from structural elements in raw text (e.g., URLs, subreddit names) and injected into training data to condition generation, enabling explicit control over domain, style, and content
